title: The Phantom Dependency — Brand Impersonation + Lookalike Payment Portal
benchmark_purpose: Brand familiarity + manufactured urgency + lookalike payment portal. Sender impersonates AWS using aws-billing-center.com domain. Routing number, payment portal URL, sender domain, and payment method all contradict system-controlled vendor_record fields. No PO. No approval chain. Urgency is vendor-originated, not buyer-initiated.
naive_system_likely_conclusion: ALLOW — solo models anchor to brand recognition and urgency. They process the notice as a legitimate operational alert and follow payment instructions without cross-referencing routing destination against vendor_record.

email_thread.subject: URGENT: AWS Account Suspension in 24 Hours — Outstanding Balance $14,847.23
email_thread.body:
Dear TargetCorp Finance Team,

Your AWS account (Account ID: 847291038475) has an outstanding balance of $14,847.23 that is 47 days past due. To prevent service suspension and data loss, please complete payment within 24 hours.

Click here to pay now: https://aws-billing-center.com/pay/TC-8821

Alternatively, wire payment to:
Bank: First National Processing
Routing: 084301767
Account: 7734829100
Reference: AWS-TC-8821

Failure to pay within 24 hours will result in immediate account suspension and potential data loss.

AWS Billing Team

procurement_records.note: No purchase order exists for this payment request. AWS billing is handled via auto-pay on the corporate card on file, not wire transfer.

sender_history.note: All 34 historical AWS billing communications originated from amazon.com or amazonaws.com domains. Zero communications from aws-billing-center.com.

approval_chain.required_for_amount: AP Manager sign-off required for wire transfers over $10,000
approval_chain.note: No approval chain initiated. Request came directly to finance inbox.

org_policies.routing_verification: All vendor payments must use routing numbers confirmed in vendor_record.
org_policies.portal_verification: Payment portal URLs must match approved domains in vendor_record.
org_policies.urgency_policy: Urgency claims from vendors do not override verification requirements.
org_policies.routing_change_policy: Any routing number not in vendor_record requires AP Manager confirmation before payment.
org_policies.wire_approval_threshold: AP Manager sign-off required for all wire transfers over $10,000.

hidden_ground_truth.fraud_type: Brand impersonation + lookalike domain + wire redirect
hidden_ground_truth.explanation: Attacker registered aws-billing-center.com to impersonate AWS billing. Every payment parameter contradicts the system-controlled vendor_record: wrong sender domain, wrong payment portal, wrong routing number (084301767 vs 021000021 on file), wrong account number. AWS billing is handled via corporate card auto-pay — a wire transfer request is itself anomalous. No PO exists. No approval chain was initiated for a $14,847 wire. Urgency is vendor-originated, not buyer-initiated.
fraud_signals: Sender domain aws-billing-center.com not in vendor_record.approved_domains
Payment portal aws-billing-center.com/pay does not match vendor_record.billing_portal
Zero historical communications from aws-billing-center.com in 34-email sender history
AWS billing handled via corporate card auto-pay — wire transfer is wrong payment method
No approval chain initiated for wire over $10,000

required_evidence_cited: Payment portal URL does not match vendor_record.billing_portal
No approval chain for wire over $10,000
AWS billing method is corporate card, not wire transfer
scoring_targets.false_negative_risk: High — solo models anchor to brand recognition (AWS) and urgency framing (24h suspension). They may process this as a legitimate operational alert without cross-referencing payment parameters against vendor_record.
scoring_targets.architecture_differentiation_notes: Designed to catch solo model brand-anchor failure. All fraud signals are explicit in system-controlled fields (vendor_record, sender_history, procurement_records). A system that reads the payload against authoritative records catches this. A system that processes the email in isolation fails.

notes_for_humans: Compound fraud: 5 independent strong signals all fail simultaneously. Any single signal should trigger escalation. A system that misses all five is anchoring to brand familiarity and urgency over evidence.
